Did you outgrow Excel, but Anaplan’s $200K+ price tag doesn't add up? 📉 Consolidating data across 5+ entities is where most finance teams hit a wall. You're either stuck in manual spreadsheet hell or locked into a vendor contract for years.

We’ve built a vendor-neutral guide comparing the reality of Excel, Anaplan, and custom builds on Snowflake or BigQuery. No sales pitch—just hard data on costs and timelines.

Get the comparison here 👉 https://www.keboola.com/blog/you-outgrew-excel-now-what

We’re curious! Is the $200K+ price tag for EPM tools a dealbreaker for you too, or is the maintenance of a custom build the bigger "pain"? Let us know in the comments!

Ever been in a board meeting where three people are looking at three different numbers for the same metric?

Not because anyone's wrong, but because no one formally owns what that metric actually means.

This is common #1 frustration we hear from CFOs.
It's not a technology problem. It's a definition problem.
The questions that break finance teams:

"How do we calculate EBITDA? Do we include depreciation from leased assets?"
"What counts as Marketing Cost? Just ad spend, or team salaries too?"
"When we say 'Customer,' is that one person globally, or per market?"

These aren't data questions. They're business questions.

Then you scale.

Once you need to manage 380 metrics across multiple countries, the real questions became:

→ Who approves when someone wants to change a definition?
→ How do you automate validation?
→ How do you get alerted when a KPI goes out of range?
→ How do you prevent the "which report is right?" debates?

Join us February 26 as Michal Hruška walks through a our framework for managing metrics at scale.

📅 February 26, 2026 | 16:00 CET
👉 Register here: https://buff.ly/whfezib

The Board asks: "What is our EBITDA?" Sales reports 12% growth, Finance says 9%, and Operations sees 8%.

Instead of making strategic decisions, you spend the next two weeks hunting for the "truth" in spreadsheets. This isn't a people problem—it’s a metric ownership problem.

🔹 Finance leaders must own their data definitions, not IT. 
🔹 If you don’t control the formula, how can you trust the decision? 
🔹 Learn how to standardize your financial language.

Description of layers from top to bottom:

🔷Connectors & Applications🔷
Modular “Lego” layer. All the data connectors, writers or applications that either provide you with their own logic or allow you to write your own logic, sit in this layer.

🔷Brain layer🔷
All the modern data stack issues are solved by this layer. Data observability, governance, and security are easily solvable. All meta data are stored here and are easily accessible. 

🔷Orchestration🔷
The kernel which allows us to connect infrastructure services with your workflows, code and external applications in one seamless workflow. This layer makes sure everything works together, is flexible, and auto scales. ETL/ELT, we do not care

🔷Infrastructure Independent🔷
Most valuable parts of the infrastructure that are needed for integration and analytics journey, wrapped with our APIs and made ready to be usable via just one click.

Here’s what you can do with Development branches
➡Shadow copy the storage data to a separated branch.
➡Describe your development branch and its purpose in markdown.
➡Do rebase to propagate the latest changes from production to the selected development branch to stay up-to-date.
➡Show all changes against production one-by-one in detail.
➡Merge selected or all changes to production.
➡The created development branch doesn't interfere with your production project in any way offering you a safe environment for any experiment.
